trm:term.trm:name: Merge Strategy
trm:term.trm:definition: A pluggable algorithm for resolving file-level merge conflicts. Strategies implement the MergeStrategy trait and return either a resolved content hash or a divergence signal. Built-in strategies are Ours, Theirs, AutoMerge, and Manual.

trm:term.trm:name: Merge Policy
trm:term.trm:definition: A dispatcher that selects a merge strategy per file path. Holds a default strategy and optional per-file overrides matched by path suffix. Enables different resolution approaches for different parts of a specification.

trm:term.trm:name: Child Key
trm:term.trm:definition: An identity key assigned to each child node in the Merkle DAG for three-way merge matching. Elements with an id attribute are keyed by (local_name, id_value); elements without id are keyed by (local_name, positional_index). Text, comment, and PI nodes are keyed by type-specific occurrence index. This prevents cascading mismatches when children are inserted or deleted on different sides.

trm:term.trm:name: Divergence
trm:term.trm:definition: A conflict representation stored as a sidecar document under .clayers/divergence/ in the repository tree. Contains the ancestor version and both sides' content as repo:divergence XML. The original document stays valid at its path (keeping ours' version). The divergence filename encodes a hash of the three sides to allow multiple unresolved conflicts per file. Resolution is a subsequent commit that removes the divergence entry.

trm:term.trm:name: Merge Outcome
trm:term.trm:definition: The result of a merge operation on a repository. Four variants: FastForward (target was ahead, HEAD moved forward), Merged (a merge commit with two parents was created), UpToDate (nothing to do), and NoCommonAncestor (disjoint histories).

pr:section.pr:title: Merge Framework
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Three-way merge with pluggable strategies for XML documents in the content-addressed Merkle DAG.
pr:section.pr:p: The merge framework provides file-level and element-level three-way merge for divergent branches. Given a common ancestor (found via LCA in the commit graph), two trees are compared to the ancestor to classify each file path as unchanged, one-side-changed, convergent, or conflicting. Conflicts are routed to a pluggable strategy for resolution.
Merge commits have two parents, recording both lineages in the DAG. Objects are immutable: merge creates new tree and commit objects rather than modifying existing ones.

pr:section.pr:title: Strategy Framework
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Trait-based strategy dispatch with per-file overrides.
pr:section.pr:p: The MergeStrategy trait defines a single async resolve method that receives a MergeConflict (path, ancestor, ours, theirs, ref names, deletion flags) and returns either a resolved content hash or a divergence signal. Four built-in implementations cover common resolution approaches: Ours always takes our version, Theirs always takes theirs, Manual always produces divergence markers, and AutoMerge attempts element-level three-way merge before falling back to divergence. All strategies treat delete-vs-modify conflicts as divergence: when one side deletes a file and the other modifies it, no strategy silently resurrects the deleted file.

MergePolicy dispatches strategies per file path. It holds a default strategy and optional overrides matched by path suffix, enabling different resolution approaches for different files within a single merge operation.

pr:section.pr:title: File-Level Merge
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Three-way triage of tree entries using diff_trees.
pr:section.pr:p: The merge_trees function compares ancestor, ours, and theirs tree objects by building path-to-hash maps and iterating the union of all paths. Each path is classified by comparing its hash across the three sides: unchanged (all same), one-side-changed (take that side), convergent (both changed to same hash), or conflicting (both changed differently). Delete-vs-modify conflicts are detected when one side removes a file and the other modifies it.

For conflicts, the configured strategy is invoked. If the strategy returns divergence, a sidecar divergence document is created and added to the merged tree alongside the original file (which retains ours' content).

pr:section.pr:title: Element-Level Merge
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Recursive three-way merge of XML elements in the Merkle DAG using identity-based child matching.
pr:section.pr:p: When AutoMerge encounters a file-level conflict, it delegates to merge_documents which loads the three document objects, extracts their root elements, and calls merge_elements recursively. merge_elements exploits the Merkle property for short-circuiting: if ours equals theirs, return either; if one side equals the ancestor, return the other side.

When all three sides differ, the algorithm merges attributes per-attribute (three-way comparison by local name and namespace URI) and children per-child (using ChildKey for identity-based matching). Children are keyed by id attribute when present, or by (local_name, positional_index) otherwise. The merged child list preserves ours' ordering with theirs-only additions appended.

Merged elements are stored as new objects in the DAG. Their hashes are computed by collecting the full subtree into a map, building the XML representation via the export pipeline, and hashing it with hash_element_xml.

pr:section.pr:title: Sidecar Divergence
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Conflict documents stored alongside originals to keep spec files valid during resolution.
pr:section.pr:p: When a conflict cannot be auto-resolved, the merge framework creates a divergence document at .clayers/divergence/path.hash in the merged tree. The original file keeps ours' content and remains valid XML. The divergence document wraps the ancestor and both sides' content in a repo:divergence element.

The divergence filename encodes a hash of all three sides' content hashes (16 hex characters), ensuring uniqueness when multiple merges conflict on the same file. tree_has_divergences and list_divergence_entries provide tree-level conflict detection by scanning for entries under the DIVERGENCE_PREFIX.

Resolution follows a simple workflow: edit the original file to the desired content, remove the divergence file, stage and commit. The merge commit with divergence entries remains in history permanently (append-only DAG).

pr:section.pr:title: CLI Command
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: clayers merge with strategy selection and conflict reporting.
pr:section.pr:p: The merge subcommand accepts a target branch name and an optional --strategy flag (auto, ours, theirs, manual). It discovers the repository, resolves the author, constructs a MergePolicy, and delegates to Repo::merge. After the merge, it exports the merged tree to disk and refreshes the working copy table. Conflict output includes the divergence sidecar paths. The command exits non-zero when unresolved conflicts remain.

org:concept.org:purpose: Provides the top-level architecture for three-way merge in the content-addressed VCS.
Defines the pluggable strategy abstraction and built-in implementations.
Describes the file-level triage that classifies each path before delegating to strategies.
Describes the recursive element-level merge that enables auto-merging of non-overlapping edits within a single XML document.
Explains the sidecar approach to conflict storage that keeps original documents valid.

org:reference.org:purpose: Documents the CLI interface for invoking merges.

rel:relation.rel:note: Strategies are the pluggable resolution mechanism within the merge framework.
File-level merge is the first tier of the merge algorithm.
Element-level merge is the second tier, invoked for file-level conflicts by AutoMerge.
Sidecar divergence is the conflict representation mechanism within the framework.
Merge reuses the commit graph (LCA), diff, and conflict infrastructure built for drift detection.
Element hashes for merged objects are computed via C14N through the export pipeline.
The CLI command is the user-facing interface to the merge framework.

spec:clayers.llm:node: Three-way merge framework for the content-addressed XML VCS. Finds the lowest common ancestor of two branches, compares file-level trees to classify paths as unchanged/one-side/convergent/conflict, then delegates conflicts to pluggable strategies (Ours/Theirs/AutoMerge/Manual). AutoMerge recursively merges XML elements using identity-based child matching (ChildKey). Creates merge commits with two parents. Unresolved conflicts are stored as sidecar divergence documents under .clayers/divergence/ so original files stay valid.

MergeStrategy trait with async resolve method returning Resolution (Resolved hash or Divergence signal). MergePolicy dispatches per file path with default + overrides. Built-in: Ours (take ours), Theirs (take theirs), Manual (always diverge), AutoMerge (element-level merge, falls back to divergence). AutoMerge detects delete-vs-modify and rejects without ancestor as immediate divergence.

merge_trees function: builds path-to-hash maps from ancestor/ours/theirs TreeObjects, iterates union of paths. Classifies: unchanged (same hash), one-side-changed (take it), convergent (both same), conflict (strategy). Handles additions, deletions, delete-vs-modify. Builds merged TreeObject, stores it, returns MergeResult with auto_merged/conflicts/ours_only/ theirs_only lists.

merge_elements: recursive async function on ContentHash triples. Short- circuits via Merkle equality. Loads ElementObjects, merges attributes (per local_name+ns_uri), keys children via ChildKey (ElementById for @id, ElementByPos for positional, Text/Comment/Other by type index). Recurses on both-changed children. Builds new ElementObject, hashes via export pipeline (collect_subtree + build_xml_from_objects + hash_element_xml), stores with correct identity and inclusive hashes.

Sidecar divergence: conflicts stored at .clayers/divergence/{path}.{hash} in the tree. Original file keeps ours' content (valid XML). Hash is SHA-256 of concatenated ancestor+ours+theirs content hashes, truncated to 16 hex chars. tree_has_divergences and list_divergence_entries scan tree entries for DIVERGENCE_PREFIX. Resolution: edit original, remove sidecar, commit. Merge commit with divergences stays in history.

CLI: clayers merge <branch> [--strategy ours|theirs|auto|manual] [-m message]. Discovers repo, resolves author, builds MergePolicy, calls Repo::merge. Exports merged tree to disk via export_merged_tree. Reports auto-merged files, ours/theirs-only, conflicts with divergence paths. Exits 1 on unresolved conflicts.

Async trait for file-level conflict resolution. resolve() takes store and MergeConflict, returns Resolution::Resolved(hash) or Resolution::Divergence. Object-safe via async_trait. Four built-in implementations: Ours, Theirs, Manual, AutoMerge.
Strategy dispatcher. default field + file_overrides vec of (path_suffix, strategy). strategy_for(path) checks overrides first, falls back to default. Stored as Box<dyn MergeStrategy>.

Enum for identity-based child matching in Merkle DAG three-way merge. Variants: ElementById(name, id), ElementByPos(name, idx), Text(idx), Comment(idx), Other(idx). key_children async function loads objects from store to assign keys. Matches @id (no namespace or xml:id namespace).

Conflict sidecar stored at .clayers/divergence/{path}.{hash} in tree. Contains repo:divergence XML with ancestor and both sides. Original file unchanged. Hash from ContentHash::from_canonical of concatenated side hashes. Detected by tree_has_divergences scanning entry paths.

Enum: FastForward { commit }, Merged { commit, result: MergeResult }, UpToDate, NoCommonAncestor. MergeResult contains tree hash, auto_merged/conflicts/ours_only/theirs_only path lists. FileConflict has path, divergence_path, conflict_count, description.

art:mapping.art:note: MergeOutcome, MergeResult, FileConflict, DIVERGENCE_PREFIX, tree_has_divergences, list_divergence_entries, divergence_entry_path.
Resolution enum, MergeConflict struct, MergeStrategy trait, MergePolicy struct.
Ours, Theirs, Manual, AutoMerge strategy implementations.
ChildKey enum and key_children async function.
merge_documents, merge_elements recursive algorithm, collect_subtree, merge_attributes.
Merge variant in the Command enum and dispatch in run().
Tests verifying all strategies produce divergence for delete-vs-modify conflicts (AutoMerge, Ours, Theirs).
